Motion For Order On The Stipulation For Filing A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Benjamin Drake, Esq. 213-381-1156of Drake Law Firm for Plaintiff through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. Plaintiff submits to the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LUIS GANTE's Motion For Order On The Stipulation For Filing A Second Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Luis Gante's motion to file a second amended complaint is denied. While California has a liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings, that policy does not apply to this motion. Mr. Gante has known about the facts supporting his request for punitive damages since before this case was filed and apparently has desired to make such a request since the outset of this case. Yet he twice moved for and received a continuance of the trial without moving to amend his complaint to seek punitive damages and now seeks to do so less than two weeks before trial. Granting this motion would be prejudicial to defendant Hui Wu since there is insufficient time before the trial to take discovery and adjudicate motions pertaining to the request for punitive damages. The court expresses no view whether the allegation that an alleged violation of Vehicle Code 23123(a) is sufficient to allege a cognizable request for punitive damages.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
